title: Apply: humans and ecosystems
description: Apply your understanding of humans and ecosystems in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Humans get many resources from desert ecosystems. Which of these desert resources provides humans with a source of usable energy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: sunlight
gemstones
cacti for gardens

assessment_items.question_content:
**Humans get many resources from rainforest ecosystems. Which of these rainforest resources provides humans with a source of medicine?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: plants that make anti-cancer compounds
trees that can be used for building materials
orchids that can be used as houseplants

assessment_items.question_content:
**Humans get many resources from ocean ecosystems. Which of these ocean resources provides humans with a source of food?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: fish that are rich in healthy fats
sea sponges that make anti-cancer compounds
shells that can be used for jewelry

assessment_items.question_content:
Acadia National Park in Maine contains many large areas of wetlands. These wetlands are home to many animals and plants. They also help block the flow of dirt and pollutants to nearby coastal ecosystems.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which two of these statements about Acadia National Park describe ecosystem services?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: "[Bass Harbor Marsh](https://www.flickr.com/photos/63341435@N05/5768866718)" by National Parks Explorer, Raymond Doherty, [CC BY-NC-SA 2.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/2.0/)

options.alt: A flat area of water and grass surrounded by pine trees.
options.caption: *Acadia National Park wetlands*

choices.content: Populations of fish that people catch and sell are kept healthy by the wetlands.
People can pick the wetland’s sweetgrass and use it to make baskets.
The wetlands are at risk from pollution, which has harmful effects on the organisms that live there.

assessment_items.question_content:
The wetlands along the coast of Louisiana can stretch inland from the sea for up to 60 miles. These wetlands are where young shrimp, crabs, and fish spend their time. The wetlands are also a stop for migrating birds.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which two of these statements about Louisiana’s wetlands describe ecosystem services?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: "[Sabine Wetlands Walk, Hackberry, La](https://www.flickr.com/photos/51104607@N02/4716171712)" by louisianatravel, [CC BY-ND 2.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nd/2.0/)

options.alt: A flat area of grass and water.
options.caption: *Louisiana wetlands*

choices.content: The wetlands help protect coastal towns from large waves during storms.
People can visit the wetlands and enjoy seeing many species of birds.
The wetlands are at risk of disappearing due to rising sea levels.

assessment_items.question_content:
Florida has over 1,000 square miles of mangrove forests. These forests contain mangrove trees, which are adapted to live in the salty water of the coastline. The mangrove forests are home to many species of fish, birds, and mammals.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which two of these statements about Florida’s mangrove forests describe ecosystem services?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: "Mangrove tree inside Snipes Point near Key West](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mangrove_tree_inside_Snipes_Point_near_Key_West.jpg)" by Florida Memory, [public domain](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Public_domain)

options.alt: Trees with tangled roots growing in salt water along a coastline.
options.caption: *Florida mangroves*

choices.content: The mangrove forests help protect coastal towns from damage during large storms.
People can enjoy tours through the mangrove forests to see endangered species, such as manatees.
The mangrove forests are under threat from rising sea levels.

assessment_items.question_content:
In a certain coral reef ecosystem, over half of the corals have died off in recent years. As a result, the reef has lost much of its biodiversity.

**What will be a likely result of this loss of biodiversity?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It will be harder for local people to catch a variety of seafood to eat.
Income from tourists visiting the coral reef will increase.
The ecosystem will become healthier because there will be fewer corals using up resources.

assessment_items.question_content:
In a certain freshwater lake ecosystem, chemicals from nearby farms have entered the water. These chemicals have caused an overgrowth of algae in the lake. As a result, the lake has lost much of its biodiversity.

**What will be a likely result of this loss of biodiversity?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It will be harder for local people to catch a variety of lake fish to eat.
People will use the lake as a source of drinking water.
The ecosystem will become healthier because algae can produce oxygen through photosynthesis.

assessment_items.question_content:
In a certain river ecosystem, the water has become more acidic. This happened because of air pollution that came into the river via rain. As a result, the river has lost much of its biodiversity.

**What will be a likely result of this loss of biodiversity?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It will be harder for local people to catch a variety of fish from the river.
People will use the river as a source of drinking water.
The ecosystem will become healthier because pollution has been taken out of the air.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which action would most likely protect a forest’s ecosystem services?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: creating a network of hiking trails in the forest
allowing hunting to occur in the forest without rules
removing an area of trees to build a shopping center

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which action would most likely protect a grassland’s ecosystem services?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: making the grassland a protected area of wilderness
expanding a city so that part of it is built in the grassland
allowing hunting to occur in the grassland without rules

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which action would most likely protect a tundra’s ecosystem services?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: creating a park that makes the tundra a protected area
mining for natural resources in the tundra
removing tundra habitat to build a town
